summary: Large miscellaneous Dolphin-library matching PR that replaced multiple small asm stubs and non-matching blocks with C across dolphin/os, dolphin/gx, and dolphin/ax. It removed AX.s in favor of AX.c, deleted OSRtc_data.s and OSTime_data.s after moving data into C, typed several OS/GX structures and callback signatures, renamed anonymous lbl globals to meaningful symbols, and updated obj_files.mk accordingly. The PR text is sparse; the only explicit motivation was to merge these matches so more matches could be ported from Prime, with a reviewer/commenter suggesting shared Dolphin code across projects.
what_changed: Replaced deleted asm/dolphin/ax/AX.s with src/dolphin/ax/AX.c implementing AXInitEx as a straight sequence of __AXAllocInit, __AXVPBInit, __AXSPBInit, __AXAuxInit, __AXClInit, and __AXOutInit calls.
Updated obj_files.mk to build src/dolphin/ax/AX.c.o instead of asm/dolphin/ax/AX.s.o and to stop building deleted OSRtc_data.s.o and OSTime_data.s.o.

Promoted several Dolphin OS data symbols from anonymous labels to named globals: lbl_804A7C80 became Ecb, lbl_804D7378 became InterruptHandlerTable, lbl_804D73D8 became RunQueueBits, and lbl_804D73E0 became Reschedule.
Deleted asm/dolphin/os/OSRtc_data.s and moved its SRAM/control-buffer storage into OSRtc.c as static C storage such as Scb and lbl_804A7DA0.
Deleted asm/dolphin/os/OSTime_data.s and moved the calendar day tables into OSTime.c as static YearDays and LeapYearDays arrays, along with C helper logic for leap years and calendar conversion.

Converted or improved C matches in GX files, including __GXSetVAT, __GXDefaultTlutRegionCallback, GXLoadTexObj, GXSetTexRegionCallback, GXSetTlutRegionCallback, and matrix write inline-asm helpers in GXTransform.c.
Expanded src/dolphin/gx/__types.h so GXTexRegionCallback returns void*, and refined GXContext layout around x2D0/x410/x414 to support TLUT and texture-region callback matches.
Changed GXSetNumTevStages to cast the packed callback-word manipulation back to GXTexRegionCallback instead of a generic void function pointer.
Converted OSGetConsoleType and ClearArena in OS.c from raw asm/unk_t style to typed C using an OSBootInfo struct and absolute boot-region symbols.

Added typed declarations and C wrappers in OSAlarm.c, OSContext.c, OSInterrupt.c, OSMemory.c, OSReset.c, OSReboot.c, OSRtc.c, OSSerial.c, OSThread.c, and OSTime.c while retaining remaining asm where not yet matched.
Changed include/dolphin/os/OSInterrupt.h so __OSSetInterruptHandler and __OSGetInterruptHandler take s16 rather than OSInterruptType, matching the implementation signature used in this PR.
smart_moves: Moved matched data out of standalone asm data files only when the owning C file had enough typed structure to hold it, reducing object-list clutter without hiding layout assumptions.
Used meaningful Dolphin SDK-style global names for known data symbols while leaving weakly understood symbols as lbl_* where evidence was not yet strong.
Improved GXContext layout just enough to express callbacks and TLUT regions in C, enabling several small GX functions to match naturally instead of staying as hand-written asm.
Localized #pragma peephole off around matching-sensitive code paths instead of globally altering compiler behavior.

For GXTransform paired-single helpers, used register float* inline-asm parameters and symbolic operands rather than hard-coded r3/r4 comments, making inline asm easier to maintain while preserving instruction selection.
Matched __GXSetVAT by using an explicit unsigned cast in the shift expression, `1 << (u32) i`, a small source-level change that removed the need for a large asm fallback.
Replaced tiny call-sequence asm with simple C when the function semantics were obvious, as in AXInitEx and GXLoadTexObj.
Introduced typed volatile absolute externs for hardware or low-memory addresses in OS code, making memory-mapped accesses clearer than raw pointer casts.
decomp_lessons: Small type corrections can unlock matches: callback return types, signedness of interrupt IDs, and pointer/struct field types changed codegen enough to replace asm.
Anonymous data labels should be renamed when the SDK or surrounding code makes the role clear; otherwise preserve lbl_* names rather than overclaiming knowledge.
When deleting asm data files, also update obj_files.mk in the same PR so object ordering and build inputs stay coherent.
Matching Dolphin library code benefits from cross-project comparison, but the PR evidence only explicitly mentions Prime as a source of more matches, not exact copied code.
GX callback arrays may be represented as function-pointer arrays even when some code also treats callback words as packed register fields; use explicit casts at mutation sites.
Inline asm can be made more maintainable by using C function signatures and register parameters while still emitting paired-single instructions exactly.
For MWCC matching, #pragma peephole off may be required around functions following inline asm; document and scope it carefully.
Replacing `unk_t` externs with concrete structs, typedefs, and function pointer signatures often has matching value beyond readability.
naming_conventions: Use CamelCase or SDK-like names for identified OS globals: Ecb, InterruptHandlerTable, RunQueueBits, Reschedule.
Use OS-prefixed concrete types for Dolphin OS structs and callbacks, e.g. OSBootInfo, OSResetFunctionInfo, OSResetQueue, OSAlarmHandler.
Use GX-prefixed callback typedefs for GX internals, e.g. GXTexRegionCallback.
Static file-local data migrated from asm should be named descriptively when known, e.g. YearDays and LeapYearDays; uncertain arrays may remain lbl_*.
Hardware absolute externs were named after their SDK/role where possible, such as Mem_Size, Simulated_Mem, BOOT_REGION_START, BOOT_REGION_END, and __DIRegs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace asm-only leaf wrappers with equivalent C when the call order and arguments are obvious, as done for AXInitEx.
Use `(1 << (u32) i)` rather than `(1 << i)` in __GXSetVAT to influence shift codegen and match the original.
Use typed function-pointer callback arrays and return the old callback in setter functions to match GXSetTexRegionCallback and GXSetTlutRegionCallback.
Use `#pragma peephole off` for matching-sensitive functions where MWCC peephole behavior differs, especially around GXInit/OS code and functions following inline asm.
Represent low-memory and hardware registers as volatile absolute externs instead of repeated raw address dereferences.
Use register-qualified inline asm parameters for paired-single matrix helper functions, preserving source readability while keeping exact `psq_l`, `psq_st`, `lfs`, and `stfs` sequences.
Move asm data tables into static C arrays when the table contents are known and order-sensitive, as with OSTime calendar day tables.

review_feedback.feedback: Would be nice to move these over to a shared repo with at least Prime.
review_feedback.author_response: Will definitely look into this. Merging this so I can port over more matches from Prime.
review_feedback.lesson: Dolphin library decomp work was considered reusable across decomp projects; future shared-code or cross-project porting infrastructure would reduce duplicated matching work.

review_feedback.feedback: No line-level review comments were present in the provided slice.
review_feedback.lesson: Evidence for reviewer intent beyond the shared-repo/Prime comment is weak.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that moved Dolphin matches into a shared repo or coordinated with Prime.
Search for subsequent cleanup of GXTexRegionCallback signature and GXContext x2D0/x410/x414 field names.
Search for later PRs that rename remaining OSRtc lbl_804A7DA0 or OSReset unknown structs.
Check whether __OSSetInterruptHandler s16 signature persisted or was later changed back to OSInterruptType with casts.
Find other PRs using `(1 << (u32) i)` or similar casts as a matching tactic.
Find later removals of #pragma peephole off around GXTransform/GXInit/OS functions.
